description: A MethodChannel call assumes the native handler exists, crashing with MissingPluginException on a platform that lacks it.
body: Failure mode: Dart invokes a platform-channel method that is only implemented on one platform (or not at all on web/desktop), so on the unsupported platform the call throws MissingPluginException and the feature crashes instead of degrading. Detection signals: MissingPluginException/PlatformException at runtime; invokeMethod with no platform branch or error handling; a native handler implemented for Android but not iOS (or vice versa). Do not call a channel without confirming support. Safer fallback: implement the handler on every supported native side, branch on platform or feature-detect before invoking, catch MissingPluginException/PlatformException to degrade gracefully, and cover the unsupported path in a mocked-channel test.
